q: The teachers were present at the meeting. The headmaster was present at the meeting. (Use 'both ...... and'.) 2024
a: Both the teachers and the headmaster were present at the meeting.

q: I have a regular job and a family life and so does my friend. (Use 'both...and'.) 2024
a: Both my friend and I have regular jobs and family lives.

q: Active listening plays an essential role in effective collaboration. Problem-solving also plays an essential role in effective collaboration. (Join the sentences, using 'both ... and'.) 2025
a: Both active listening and problem-solving play an essential role in effective collaboration.

q: Rope skipping is an enjoyable activity for children. Hoop rolling is an enjoyable activity for children, too. (Join the sentences, using 'both ... and'.) 2025
a: Both rope skipping and hoop rolling are enjoyable activities for children.

q: The movie is being shown at Globe Theatre. It is also being shown at neighbourhood theatres. (Join the sentences, using 'both ... and'.) 2025
a: The movie is being shown both at Globe Theatre and at neighbourhood theatres.

q: Zaw Zaw enjoys hiking in the mountains. Nandar also enjoys hiking in the mountains. (Join the sentences, using 'both...and'.) 2025
a: Both Zaw Zaw and Nandar enjoy hiking in the mountains.

q: The director of the film wanted to win recognition. He also wanted to receive recognition. (Join the sentences, using 'both...and'.) 2026
a: The director of the film wanted both to win and to receive recognition.
